common.providerNotConfigured: Add a provider API key to get started.
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Providers · models · keys
settings.memoryNote: Held in memory for this session only — not persisted, never logged. Sent only to {{provider}} to run your requests.
settings.keyBadPrefix: That doesn't look like a {{provider}} key.
settings.keyRejected: This key was rejected — check it and save again.
settings.keysMemoryFooter: Keys live in memory for this session only.
settings.noKeyTitle: No key needed — runs on this device
settings.noKeyBody: Served locally via Ollama. Your text never leaves the machine — the privacy-preserving path.
settings.privacyLocal: Nothing leaves this device. Requests run against your local Ollama server.
settings.keyOptional: Optional — leave blank for a keyless endpoint
settings.connIdleDetail: Run a test to check this provider
settings.customEmptyBlurb: Point Lucid at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — your gateway, a proxy, a self-host.
settings.customKeyMemoryNote: The key is held in memory for this session only — never written to disk. Leave it empty for keyless local endpoints.
error.duplicateLabel: A provider named "{{label}}" already exists. Pick a unique label.
settings.removeCustomBody: Its endpoint, model and (in-memory) key are forgotten. Built-in vendors and your other customs are untouched.
settings.removeCustomActiveNotice: This is the active provider — the workspace will switch to {{fallback}}.
toolbar.subtitle: Translate and polish · one workspace
translate.label: Translate
translate.sourcePlaceholder: Type or paste text in Chinese or English — Lucid detects the direction…
translate.resultPlaceholder: The translation will stream in here. Lucid detects whether your text is Chinese or English and translates the other way automatically.
privacy.hosted: Your text is sent to {{provider}} to be processed.
privacy.local: Your text stays on this device — processed locally.
polish.label: Polish
polish.subtitle: refine a draft against its meaning & domain
polish.originalPlaceholder: The original sentence, in its source language…
polish.draftPlaceholder: The draft translation you want to refine…
polish.keywordsHint: tell the model which domain you're in
polish.rejectTitle: Discard the polish — keep your draft
polish.resultPlaceholder: The polished sentence will stream in here — refined for fluency while preserving the original meaning and honoring your domain keywords. Switch to Compare to see what changed.
banner.rateLimited.title: Rate limited
banner.providerDown.title: Provider unavailable
banner.invalidKey.title: Invalid API key
banner.requestFailed.title: Request failed
banner.timeout.title: Timed out
banner.aborted.title: Stopped
banner.refusal.title: Request declined
banner.incomplete.title: Response cut off
banner.validation.title: Check your input
banner.unknown.title: Something went wrong
banner.stopped.title: Stopped
banner.stopped.body: You stopped this — the partial result above is kept.
error.storageFull: Local storage is full — recent history may not be saved.
sidebar.sessionsEmpty: Sessions and their tasks will appear here as you translate and polish.
sidebar.glossaryEmpty: Saved domain terms live here, synced across sessions.
error.rateLimited: You've hit the rate limit — please wait a moment and try again.
error.providerDown: The provider is temporarily unavailable — try again or switch providers.
error.invalidKey: Your API key is missing or invalid.
error.timeout: The request timed out — please try again.
error.refusal: The model declined to respond to this request.
error.incomplete: The response was cut off before it finished.
error.validation: Please check your input and try again.
error.unknown: Something went wrong — please try again.
sync.connect.title: Connect a sync server
sync.connect.subtitle: Self-hosted · your data stays on your own box
sync.connect.optInCallout: Sync is off by default. Lucid works fully local-only — nothing leaves this device until you connect. Connecting is opt-in and reversible.
sync.connect.serverUrlHint: https only · the address where you deployed the Lucid sync server
sync.connect.tokenHint: paste the token printed by your server's setup command
sync.connect.dataScopeTitle: What leaves this device when sync is on
sync.connect.dataScopeFootnote: Sent only to your server — never to Lucid, never as analytics. Pair with a local model for a zero-third-party path.
sync.connect.persistedTokenNote: Your token is saved on this device (shown as …{{last4}}) so background sync survives reloads — a deliberate exception to the in-memory-only rule for provider keys. Transmitted over TLS only.
sync.connecting.title: Connecting…
sync.panel.title: Sync
sync.panel.subtitle: your self-hosted server
sync.panel.tokenNote: token …{{last4}} · saved on this device · TLS
sync.panel.localKeptNote: Local copy is always kept. Disconnecting at any time reverts Lucid to exactly how it behaves with no server — nothing is lost on this device.
sync.panel.revAuthorityNote: Ordering authority is the server-assigned rev, not device clocks — a skewed clock can't silently overwrite.
sync.disconnect.disconnectSub: revert to local-only · server data kept
sync.disconnect.eraseSub: also wipe everything on the server
sync.disconnect.dialogBody: Lucid reverts to local-only. Your data on this device is untouched either way.
sync.disconnect.choiceKeepSub: Stop syncing. Rows already uploaded stay on your server — reconnect later to resume.
sync.disconnect.choiceEraseSub: Also delete everything Lucid uploaded to {{server}}. Can't be undone.
sync.disconnect.eraseFailed: Disconnected, but the server data could not be erased — try again later.
sync.conflict.title: A local edit was superseded
sync.conflict.subtitle: another device's edit won the merge
sync.conflict.body: You and another device edited the same item while offline. The server kept the most recent write — your earlier version was superseded.
sync.conflict.reviewDeferred: Side-by-side review & restore arrives in a later release. For now this is a heads-up — nothing else needs doing.
sync.conflict.copyDeferred: Side-by-side review and restore arrives in a later release.
error.syncUnreachable: Can't reach your sync server — changes are queued locally and will sync once it's back.
error.syncAuth: Sync token rejected — paste a fresh token to resume syncing.
error.syncConflict: An edit was superseded — a newer change from another device won.
sync.banner.unreachableBody: No response from your sync server. Changes are queued locally and will sync once it's back. Retrying automatically.
sync.banner.authBody: Your server returned 401. The token may have been rotated or revoked. Paste a fresh token to resume syncing.
sync.banner.conflictBody: A newer change from another device won. Details saved.
sync.dialog.title: Sync
sync.dialog.description: Connect a self-hosted sync server, or manage your existing connection.
autorun.disabledReason: Add a key for {{provider}} in Settings to enable auto-run.
autorun.cost.title: Auto-run uses a paid provider
autorun.cost.body: You're on {{provider}}. Auto-run will spend tokens each time your text settles. A local model has no per-run cost.
autorun.paused.title: Auto-run paused
autorun.paused.body: The provider lost its key, so nothing is running on its own. Your text and toggle are kept — fix the key and auto-run resumes.
configSync.unlock.title: Unlock your config
configSync.unlock.found: encrypted blob found · synced from another device
configSync.unlock.body: Enter your passphrase to decrypt your providers & config on this device. It never leaves this machine.
configSync.set.title: Encrypt & sync your config
configSync.set.subtitle: first device · choose a passphrase
configSync.set.body: This passphrase encrypts your providers, models and API keys with end-to-end encryption before they sync. You'll enter it once on each new device.
configSync.set.noRecoveryBody: Lose this passphrase and your synced config can't be decrypted — not by you, not by us. The server only ever holds ciphertext. Store it in a password manager.
configSync.set.privacyPassphrase: Passphrase — stays in memory, never stored
configSync.set.privacyNote: Key is derived from the passphrase (no key re-entry on unlock). Changing config re-encrypts & saves; the passphrase is never written to disk.
configSync.strength.label: Passphrase strength
configSync.error.wrongPassphraseTitle: That passphrase didn't decrypt the config.
configSync.error.wrongPassphraseBody: Check it and try again — or the blob may be corrupt.
configSync.error.unreachableBody: The server hosting your encrypted config didn't respond. Your local workspace still works — unlock will resume once it's reachable.
configSync.error.requestFailedBody: The server was reached but the request errored. Nothing was decrypted. Try again, or check the server logs.
configSync.insecure.title: Secure connection required
configSync.insecure.subtitle: encrypted sync is unavailable on http://
configSync.insecure.body: End-to-end encryption needs a secure context — the browser's crypto.subtle API is unavailable over plain HTTP. Open Lucid over HTTPS to set or unlock a passphrase.
configSync.insecure.tailscaleHint: Quickest path: front Lucid with HTTPS via Tailscale.
configSync.noConfig.title: No synced config yet
configSync.noConfig.body: This device will create the first encrypted blob. Set a passphrase to turn on cross-device sync.
configSync.banner.insecureBody: You're on http:// — crypto is disabled here. Reopen Lucid over its HTTPS address (e.g. tailscale serve) to set or unlock a passphrase.
configSync.banner.wrongPassphraseBody: The passphrase was wrong, or the stored blob is corrupt. Re-enter it to try again — nothing on this device changed.
configSync.banner.unreachableBody: No response. Retrying automatically — local works.
configSync.banner.requestFailedBody: Server reached but returned an error. Nothing decrypted.
